title: ERP Professional Services: 1998 Practice Summary
author: Aberdeen Group
abstract: Aberdeen Group's 1998 practice summary covering the ERP professional services market, classifying providers into OEMs (Compaq, HP), ISVs (SAP, Oracle, J.D. Edwards, PeopleSoft, Baan), and Independent Professional Services Providers (IPSPs) including Big Six consultancies and specialist firms. The report categorizes ERP services into Planning, Implementation, Project Management, and Lifecycle Support, and profiles 14 suppliers with competitive assessments. Key findings highlight declining implementation costs due to new methodologies, Y2K-driven ERP adoption, and growing merger/acquisition activity among IPSPs.
importance_rationale: Provides a contemporary vendor landscape snapshot of the late-1990s ERP professional services boom, documenting the Big Six consulting firms' ERP practices and mid-market specialist firms at a pivotal moment before major consolidations (PwC-Coopers merger, Compaq-Digital).
relevance_rationale: ERP professional services as a market category remains highly relevant; the vendor landscape has changed dramatically (Big Four replacing Big Six, Andersen Consulting became Accenture) but the service categorization framework and competitive dynamics remain instructive.
prescience_rationale: Predicted PwC-Coopers merger emergence as premier IPSP (proved correct), Compaq-Digital integration strengthening ERP position (partially correct), SAP market leadership continuation (correct), and CTP middle-market squeeze from new entrants (proved correct).
